acceptance.reason: steady-state ratio is 0.66x (XLOG faster than Scallop); total_train_sec ratio is 32.8x (dominated by one-time first-epoch warmup)

task: MNIST addition (5 epochs, 512 train images, batch_size=64)

xlog.version: 0.4.0-alpha (batched circuit eval enabled via free-var mask fix)
xlog.source: re-run after perf(xlog-prob): skip free-var correction for all-zero masks

xlog_previous.note: Before batched eval was unblocked (sequential per-query evaluation)
xlog_previous.source: previous Track B run (pre free-var mask fix)

notes: Batched circuit evaluation unblocked by skipping all-zero free-var masks at compile time.
XLOG is now 1.5x FASTER than Scallop in steady-state (0.97ms vs 1.46ms per query).
Total train time still dominated by first-epoch warmup (71.7s CUDA JIT + circuit compilation).
At 50 epochs: projected XLOG ~83.8s vs Scallop ~19s = ~4.4x ratio (amortized warmup).
Loss convergence is identical to previous runs (same seeds, same final losses).
Accuracy (heldout_addition_acc) not re-evaluated in this run; null fields to be filled by eval pass.

hardware: NVIDIA RTX PRO 3000 Blackwell Generation Laptop GPU (12 GB, SM120)
